license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.4rdgv26
title: RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Protocol 5: pBIOZOL Method

steps_raw.instruction: Grind tissue to a powder in liquid nitrogen.
Add 1.3 mL of cold ( 4 °C ) pBIOZOL Reagent for up to 100 mg of frozen, ground tissue.
Incubate the tube for 00:05:00 at 20 °C . Lay the tube down horizontally to maximize surface area during RNA extraction.
Centrifuge for 00:02:00 at .
Add 100 µL of 5 Molarity (M) NaCl to the extract.
Add 300 µL of chloroform.
Centrifuge the sample at 4 °C for 00:10:00 at to separate the phases.
Add to the aqueous phase an equal volume of isopropyl alcohol.
Centrifuge the sample at 4 °C for 00:10:00 at .
Decant the supernatant, taking care not to lose the pellet.
Centrifuge at Room temperature for 00:05:00 at .
Add 10 µL – 30 µL RNase-free water to dissolve the RNA.
Mix by briefly vortexing or flicking the bottom of the tube until the sample is thoroughly suspended.
Transfer the supernatant to an RNase-free tube.
Tap tube to mix.
Mix thoroughly by inversion.
Transfer the top aqueous phase to an RNase-free tube.
Mix.
Let stand at 20 °C for 00:10:00 .
Add 1 mL of chilled 75 % ethanol to the pellet. Pellet may be difficult to see.
Decant the liquid carefully, taking care not to lose the pellet.
Briefly centrifuge to collect the residual liquid and remove it with a pipette.

Pipette the water up and down over the pellet to dissolve the RNA. If any cloudiness is observed, centrifuge the solution at Room temperature for 00:01:00 at and transfer the supernatant to a fresh tube.
